messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is up but showing input_errors (44). R2’s running-config forces speed=100, duplex=half and negotiation auto=false while R1’s peer is set to autonegotiation — causing the link mismatch and inbound packet loss.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Configuration mismatch: R2 is forcing 100Mbps/half-duplex with autonegotiation disabled while the peer (R1) is autonegotiating. This mismatch is the likely cause of the input_errors and packet loss.

If changes were made
- Updated the interface description on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 (added notes indicating autonegotiation intent).
- Verified interface is administratively up.

Verification: passed/failed and what remains
- Verification: FAILED.
  - Evidence: latest interface-status still shows oper up, speed 102400000, duplex null, input_errors 44. Latest running-config still shows speed value-100, duplex half, negotiation auto=false.
  - Remaining: R2 still has forced speed/duplex with autonegotiation disabled; the mismatch remains and is producing input_errors. The intended autonegotiation change was not applied/persisted.

Recommended next step
- Apply the planned fixes to R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1:
  1) negotiation auto
  2) speed auto; duplex auto
- Then re-check running-config and get_interface_status and monitor input_errors to confirm resolution.
